title: History of the ‘Phantom Swede’ and the origin of Bootlegger Cove’s name
summary: Histories of Anchorage: In its early decades, the area from Bootlegger Cove to the mouth of Chester Creek was a prime place for local deliveries of moonshine, also known as hoochinoo, squirrel whiskey or white mule.
